_source: Legend in the Mist — The Oracle (solo & co-op play). Verbatim tables from the supplement.

interpretive.text: Hard work. Yearning for more. Cycles and repetition. An innocent bystander.
Polite conflict. Bargains. Contracts and promises. A worldly person.
Contentment. Wisdom of age. Introspection. Someone wise with experience.
Pride. Skill at arms. Organized and orderly. A soldier or law enforcer.
Ugliness. Intuition. Vengeance. An outcast.
Preparation. Studied knowledge. Tradition. A magic user.
Getting lost. Wandering. Seeking a direction. Someone in distress.
Uncertainty. Wasting time. Blissful ignorance. A fool or drunkard.
Ill portents. Bad luck. Untrustworthy people. A rogue, thief, or brigand.
Seeking information. Going beyond limits. Flying. A hunter.
Journeying fast. Reliability and moderation. Overcoming hardship. A mount or transport.
Seeking opportunity. Desperation. Hunger. A lone monster or beast.
Making a mark. Boldness. Duality and dilemmas. A set of useful tools.
Reaping what you have sown. Sudden death or loss. Harvest season. A dangerous situation.
Taking up arms. Threats and fears. Physical strength. Someone threatening.
Hope. Learning. Being seen or discovered. A source of knowledge.
Weaving together. Combining forces. Complex interplay. Two threats combined against you.
Making something new. Breaking down the old. Skill at a craft. A workshop or place of business.
Ceremony. Mourning. Beauty. A priest or shaman.
Choices. Offers of help. Changes in life. A chance meeting.
Harmony. Diversions and entertainment. Optimism. An ally or old friend.
Temptation. Taint or despoilment. Dangerous locations. A mysterious stranger.
Complications. Witnessing beauty and splendor. A set or fated path. An unavoidable journey.
Increasing difficulty. Lofty goals or ideals. Treacherous landscape. An obstacle.
Detailed plans. Webs of power. The center of something sprawling. A trap.
Petty tyrants. Alluring people. Silly or decadent games. A noble.
Returning. Old promises or oaths. Undying love. A ghost of the past.
Power for its own sake. Great feats. Loss of control. Someone power hungry.
Profound, unseen, or spiritual forces. Surprise. Mystery. An ambush.
Greed. Luck. Regal bearing. Someone wealthy.
Social engagements. Formality. Oracular portents. A new person.
Many guards or soldiers. Destruction. The offers of power. A dangerous path.
Putting up defenses. Withdrawing. Hiding. Something blocking your path.
Judgement. Hard choices. Authority. A leader or ruler.
Violence. War. Politics. Two forces clash with each other.
Abdication of duties. Failure of leadership. Crisis. An opportunity.

interpretiveCols.being: Guardian of a bridge or door
Face in a wall or tree
Pair of glowing eyes in the darkness

yesno.label: ⚀⚀ Double ones
yesno.answer: Extreme No — not at all (regardless of Power).
It's complicated — a Yes with a caveat, or a No with an exception.

yesno.label: ⚅⚅ Double sixes
yesno.answer: Extreme Yes — yes, and more so! (regardless of Power).

central: The terrain… (use a Landscape Journey, location Challenge, or Vignette)
An event, occurrence, or coincidence… (use an Occasion Journey, event Challenge, or Vignette)
An underling of the main villain or major vexation…
The main villain or major vexation…
role: …physically endangers… (Aggressor or Pursuer cause bodily harm or death — Ill Tidings, or negative physical statuses)
…socially or emotionally endangers… (Aggressor or Influence ruin reputation or harm psyche — negative social or emotional statuses)
…presents a dilemma… (Influence threatens two things, only one can be prevented; roll twice on Target)
…drains, despoils, or corrupts… (Sapper deplete resources or tags; despoiled, corrupted)
…stands as a major obstacle for… (Obstacle or Mystery; Blocked)
…disrupts the peace or stability of… (Influence or Sapper; Complication or mental/emotional statuses)
…is spying on, searching for, inquiring, or researching… (Watcher; Exposure, alert, gains tags)
…is tricking, fooling, infiltrating, or entrancing… (Influence or Sapper; loss of a resource, beguiled)
…is progressing towards a crescendo to… (Countdown; use a progress Limit; roll again for its endgame, treating it as especially dangerous)
…is hunting down / has stolen… (Pursuer or Quarry; seeks to capture/imprison/possess — or — escape)
target: …something taken for granted, or the status quo.
location: At home, in a village, or a small building (Homestead & Village)
A liminal or bucolic area, beyond the towns (Outskirts & Fields)
On the roadway, highway, or major trails (On the Trail)
Off the beaten path or somewhere truly wild (In the Wilds)
At a port, out at sea, or along a river (Open Water)
Somewhere forgotten, dark, or dismal (Forsaken Places)
In a bustling area, with many people (Town & City)
In a castle, court, or other place of political power (The Castle)
Near a violent conflict (On the Battlefield)
Out in the wastelands, desert, or another desolate area (Blighted Badlands)
Down in ruins, a dungeon, or a magical laboratory (Vaults of Darkness)
unfolding: An offer, contract, inquiry, or temptation
One Hero learns of it while alone or isolated
Sudden reveal upon entering or arriving
secondary: A bystander, or a group of them (Charge)
An unrelated animal or beast (Aggressor, Charge, Quarry)
The weather, a hazard, or a trap (in a Journey, use a Vignette)
The Heroes are separated or isolated (Complication, Blocked, or an Obstacle Challenge)
A pervasive negative emotion — jealousy, anger, hopelessness (Sapper)
A group of the Central Challenge (Increase Might)
Allies, minions, or offspring of the Central Challenge (same role, or Support)
storyTags: Notable landscape features (sharp rocks, dense woods, spooky marshes, tall barley stalks)
Dangerous or useful objects (roaring bonfire, alchemy supplies, stashed weapons, crates)
People (town elder, gathering crowd, children at play)
Condition (cold winds, bright sunlight, obscuring fog, eldritch glow)
Mood (partying, somber mourning, eerie silence, desperate struggle)
Enchantments (warded-against-magic, wall of flames, supernatural gloom)
Notable structural features (battlements, rickety bridge, balcony, window)
Animal or beast (bleating calf, climbing goat, barking dog)
Something out of place (ancient statue, foreign flora, oddly-colored tile)
Sensory effects (loud noise, creepy vibes, putrid smell, blinding sunlight)

Aggressor: ATTACK — Strike defensively (give a weak harmful status, and gain parry-1 or guarded-1)
ATTACK — Strike with force (give a harmful status such as wounded)
ATTACK — Lash out with a violent strike (give a strong harmful status, but gain exposed-1)
MANEUVER — Put in a compromised position (a positional status such as prone) or block movement (Blocked)
Charge: FOLLOW — Make too much noise or distract (Exposure, remove hidden, or distracted)
FOLLOW — Suddenly be gone, separated from the Hero or wandered off (Complication)
BLUNDER — Move right into the path of danger (Complication, or gain exposed or panicked)
BLUNDER — Scream for help (Exposure, New Challenge, or give compelled-to-help)
BLUNDER — Become captured, trapped, or entangled (gain restrained)
BE HURT — Suffer harm or injury, possibly fatal (gain a harmful status, or Ill Tidings)
Countdown: GET WORSE — Disarm/lower defenses (scratch a tag or reduce a positive status) or strike with fear (intimidated or confused)
GET WORSE — Advance aggressively, harming opposition (gain progress-1 and give a harmful status)
GET WORSE — Worsen in an obvious, terrifying way (gain progress-1 and give worried or scared)
RALLY — Reinforcements arrive (New Challenge or increase Might)
BREAK OUT — Shatter through a hindrance (scratch tags or remove hindering statuses)
DEFEND — Summon a new defender (New Challenge or increase Might of defenders)
DEFEND — A defender of the process attacks (roll on Aggressor or Influence)
Influence: ENTICE — Make an impression (gain friendly or give curious)
ENTICE — Discern or reveal a secret/weakness (Exposure and gain a tag for it)
ENTICE — Attract, offering something desired (give interested or beguiled, or gain a desirable tag)
CONVINCE — Disarm resistance or sow self-doubt (scratch a tag or reduce a positive status)
CONVINCE — Strongly influence (give a compelling status such as convinced or tempted)
CONVINCE — Make a deal or press to choose (give indebted and a desired tag, or Force the Hero to Choose)
Obstacle: A possible way through is sealed, blocked, or ruined (Blocked)
Become (or be revealed) more difficult to pass (gain a status/tag like steep incline, covered in brambles, flaming-3)
Harm someone interacting with it or attempting to pass (give a harmful status)
Break a tool, exhaust a resource, or make a useful skill irrelevant (reduce a resource status or scratch a tag)
Entangle, web, paralyze, or otherwise restrain passers-through (give restrained)
Summon or spawn guardians (New Challenge, with the Aggressor or Watcher role)
Mystery: Conditions worsen, making the truth harder to reveal (gain an obscuring status/tag like foggy-2 or disturbed evidence)
A useful source of information runs dry or is exhausted (scratch a tag)
Clues are inconclusive or only part-true (Yes, But…)
The truth is slipping away (gain confounding, or give a witness tight-lipped or bewitched)
The investigators pay a price: frustrated by lack of progress, shocked by what they find, etc.
A vital clue/witness escapes (New Challenge — Quarry role) or someone repels the investigators (New Challenge — Aggressor role)
Pursuer: GIVE CHASE — Draw closer and closer (add a status on the catch or track Limit)
GIVE CHASE — Gain momentum (gain speeding-1, and add a status on the catch or track Limit)
GIVE CHASE — Overcome an obstacle (scratch an obstacle tag and add a status on the catch or track Limit)
HINDER — Force the quarry to slow or reveal itself (give hobbled/slow, or scratch a movement/mount/transport tag)
HINDER — Create an obstacle ahead or steer the chase into one (create an obstacle tag)
HINDER — Maneuver to block escape on a specific path (Blocked)
Quarry: FLEE — Move further away (add a status on the outrun or escape Limit)
FLEE — Gain momentum or mask tracks (gain speeding-1 or hidden-1, and add a status on the outrun or escape Limit)
FLEE — Clear an obstacle (scratch an obstacle tag and add a status on the outrun or escape Limit)
HINDER — Slow or confuse the pursuer (give a hindering status or scratch a tag)
HINDER — Put an obstacle in the pursuer's path (create an obstacle tag)
HINDER — Draw another party into the chase (New Challenge)
Support: AID — Empower their allies (give inspired/confident/energized, or increase their Might)
AID — Protect allies, block an attack, or create cover (give guarded/warded, cover tags like barricade, or Blocked)
AID — Heal allies or restore their strength (reduce a hindering or harmful status)
AID — Recover allies' abilities (restore a Challenge's original tags)
AID — Grant allies a new asset or ability (create a tag)
AID — Restore or replace a fallen ally (New Challenge: bring a defeated Challenge back)
Sapper: MEASURE — Identify a Hero's weakness (Exposure or give a negative tag) or break their defenses (scratch a tag)
MEASURE — Find an advantageous position or hide on the fringes (gain hidden)
WEAKEN — Harry the Hero or sap their strength (give drained/despaired/hobbled/weakened/exposed, etc.)
WEAKEN — Deplete an important resource (reduce a resource status, or give a scarcity status like low-on-arrows)
WEAKEN — Sabotage a useful ability/item/position/process (scratch a tag, reduce a positive status, or set back a progress status)
WEAKEN — Put an obstacle in a Hero's path (create an obstacle tag)
Watcher: PATROL — Notice something that makes them agitated or vigilant (gain alert or suspicious)
PATROL — Spot its quarry, or find evidence of activity (Exposure)
CLOSE IN — Remove obstacles in their path to the truth (remove a concealing tag/status like disguise, hidden, mysterious)
CLOSE IN — Close down a path of escape or evasion (Blocked)
STUDY — Reveal vital information about their quarry/plans (Exposure, and remove preparedness statuses or tags)
STUDY — Figure out their quarry's next move (Exposure and gain prepared)

consequences.title: A NEW DANGER
consequences.desc: The Hero ran into a new Challenge or a new Threat from an existing Challenge. (New Challenge, Complication, optionally give the Hero surprised)

consequences.title: COLLATERAL DAMAGE
consequences.desc: The action unintentionally affects something or someone the Hero didn't want to affect. (Give an ally a negative status or scratch their tags, Ill Tidings)
specific: Ally suffers Effects of the action

consequences.title: EXPOSURE
consequences.desc: The Hero betrays vital information to their adversaries — location, identity, intentions, or plan. (Exposure, give the Challenge an advantageous tag/status)
specific: Reroll and apply to an ally

consequences.title: EXPENDITURE
consequences.desc: The Hero expends resources (stamina, arrows, ingredients, mana, etc.). (Scratch a tag, give exhausted, or reduce a resource status)

consequences.title: HARM
consequences.desc: The Hero was harmed during the action. (Give a negative status such as bleeding, burned, upset)

consequences.title: IMPERFECT EXECUTION
consequences.desc: The Hero didn't get everything they wanted. (Yes, but…, add a negative story tag or status)

consequences.title: LOSS OF ADVANTAGE
consequences.desc: The Hero lost an advantage they had or worked for. (Remove/reduce a beneficial status or scratch beneficial tags)

consequences.title: SIDE EFFECTS
consequences.desc: The Hero's action had unexpected or unwanted results. (Create a negative scene tag, or give the Hero a negative status/tag)

consequences.title: WASTED TIME
consequences.desc: The Hero spent too long, letting other complications unfold. (Advance a progress status, Block a missed course, worsen conditions)
specific: time-passes-1 (at tier 4, roll again — much worse)

revelations.act1: The Heroes' home is facing a never-before-seen danger.
revelations.act3: The Great Danger has the power to reach its end goal and is making final preparations.

revelations.act1: The dangers are caused by a common source.
revelations.act2: Some of the greater world's dangers can also become allies, tools, lore, etc.
revelations.act3: The Great Danger has attributes that make it invincible or unstoppable.

revelations.act1: The source of the danger is nearby.
revelations.act2: The Heroes must grow in order to face the world's mighty dangers.
revelations.act3: The Great Danger has a vulnerability or weakness.

revelations.act1: The Heroes are unequipped to deal with this danger.
revelations.act2: All dangers faced so far have an immensely powerful common source — the Great Danger.
revelations.act3: Overcoming a specific Agent will weaken or expose the Great Danger in a specific way.

revelations.act1: Someone nearby has the greater knowledge needed to face the danger.
revelations.act2: Many places are threatened by the Great Danger, like the Heroes' home.
revelations.act3: A unique place, item, ability, or ally can exploit the Great Danger's weaknesses.

revelations.act1: A hidden strength or resource can help the Heroes overcome the danger.
revelations.act2: The Great Danger has noticed the Heroes and seeks to stop them.
revelations.act3: The path to overcoming the Great Danger demands an equally great price or sacrifice.

revelations.act1: A dangerous undertaking is needed to find a way to overcome the danger.
revelations.act2: The Great Danger has a specific end goal toward which it strives.
revelations.act3: A price the Hero is unwilling to pay may be traded for another, but arranging this is extremely difficult.

revelations.act1: This danger originates from a bigger threat to the Heroes' way of life.
revelations.act2: Some of the Great Danger's power is carried by powerful places, creatures, or beings — the Agents.
revelations.act3: The Great Danger will launch its final stroke imminently.

revelations.act1: A greater threat will produce more dangers if the Heroes don't rise to meet it.
revelations.act2: There is a way to overcome or circumvent one of the Great Danger's chief Agents.
revelations.act3: The Heroes are now able to approach the Great Danger and make their attempt to stop it.

items: Pick from a randomly selected Journey

profileBuilder.intro: Quickly build a Challenge Profile for something you've described in the scene.

steps.t: Count your Hero's Adventure + Greatness themes (average for a group). Reduce the Adventure count by one, OR move 1 from Greatness to Adventure. That's how many Mighty aspects the Challenge has. Decide what they are: size, numbers, skill, magic, influence, cunning, riches, etc.

Roll a d6 (at the start of an adventure roll two and take the lower; near the end take the higher). 1–5 = your CR. On a 6, increase Might (add an Adventure aspect or change an Adventure aspect to Greatness) and roll again. (Tap Roll CR below.)

Choose a hard / medium / easy way to overcome it — each becomes a Limit (harm, convince, trick, threaten, destroy, subdue, dispel, banish, catch, escape…). Max: hard = CR+1, medium = CR, easy = CR−1 (remove if 0). A crescendo/transformation gets a progress Limit of 4.
Top defenses & offensive edge (these weaken Hero actions; they aren't the Challenge's active abilities). Tags: as many as its CR. Statuses: as many tiers as its CR.

Pick the Challenge's Role and use the Challenge Action Oracle for its Threats & Consequences. Main Consequence: a status of tier = CR (or half-CR in tags, rounded up). Others: tier = half-CR rounded up (or one tag for CR 1–4, two for CR 5).
